stories.title: Meloni and Sánchez clash over EU migration crackdown
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Italy’s Giorgia Meloni clashed with Spain’s Pedro Sánchez over Madrid’s decision to legalize around 500,000 asylum-seekers , according to three EU diplomats and one EU official. Meloni challenged Sánchez during a closed-door meeting of EU leaders in Brussels on Thursday, saying she wanted a debate on migration so leaders could discuss the consequences of Spain’s move on other EU countries, according to diplomats briefed on the discussion. Sánchez responded that other

stories.title: ‘The purpose of the rule is fascism’: scientists fight back against planned Trump research cuts
stories.summary: Stand Up for Science founder says proposal to control how grants are spent would ‘dismantle US science ecosystem’ While waiting to board her flight home at Ronald Reagan Washington national airport recently, Colette Delawalla was reviewing a list of possible impacts from a proposed Trump administration rule on controlling federal money, including grants for research. Delawalla, the founder of the group Stand Up for Science, had just completed a three-day visit to Capitol Hill, where she met one

stories.title: Trump backs down on plans to slash ocean science funding
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The Trump administration is no longer planning to cut a major research program contributing to global ocean science after U.S. lawmakers passed a bill protecting its existence, POLITICO’s E&E News reported on Thursday. The U.S. National Science Foundation — a government agency that funds science in the U.S. — said it “will not proceed” with its plans to remove underwater infrastructure that collects crucial information about oceanic currents and how climate chang

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
stories.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: Feloldotta a magyar kormány a korábban blokkolt ukrán lapok tiltását
stories.summary: Még az előző kormány döntött a tiltásról, válaszként arra, hogy Ukrajna több külföldi lapot, köztük a magyar Origót és a Demokratát is blokkolta.

stories.title: Magyar: Nem népszerű szeretnék lenni Brüsszelben
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök az uniós állam- és kormányfők testületében elmondott bemutatkozó felszólalásáról írt, a csúcson először képviseli ő Magyarországot.

stories.title: Az innováció és a mesterséges intelligencia korában is számít, hol élünk
stories.summary: A tudás nem online szárnyal igazán, hanem emberi kapcsolatok hálózataiban, és ezeknek a hálózatoknak megvannak a maguk térbeli szabályai. Lengyel Balázs, a Corvinus Institute for Advanced Studies (CIAS) kutatóközpont vezető professzora arról beszélt, miért válik egyre fontosabbá a földrajzi közelség épp a távmunka és az AI világában, és hogyan árulkodnak a városi kapcsolathálózatok a társadalmi egyenlőtlenségekről.

stories.title: 27 magyar fiatalt szállító busz kapott lángra menet közben Ausztriában
stories.summary: A turistabuszon összesen több mint 30-an ültek. Senki sem sérült meg, de a busz hátulja kiégett.

stories.title: Algásodik és dobja le a festéket a Lincoln medence, amit Trump miatt kellett kékre festeni
stories.summary: Hidrogén-peroxidot öntenek a vízbe, hogy megállítsák a zöldülést.

stories.title: Büszke magyarként szidta a muszlimokat a Facebookon egy osztrák idősek otthonából, három hónap felfüggesztettet kapott
stories.summary: A 73 éves férfi Budapesten született, de 18 éves kora óta Ausztriában él, az állampolgárságot is felvette.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely egy ingyenes alapmennyiség felett sávos rendszerben emelné a vízdíjat Budapesten
stories.summary: A főpolgármester levelet írt Gajdos Lászlónak, amiben azt javasolta: kapják vissza az önkormányzatok a jogot arra, hogy ők határozhassák meg a vízdíjakat.

stories.title: Nem veszi igénybe a Pro Economica magyar közpénzből megítélt támogatását a gyergyói Maros Hotel felújítója
stories.summary: 2,22 milliárd forintnyi támogatást kapott szállodafejlesztésre, de megváltoztak a beruházásának a prioritásai, és inkább nem veszi el az összeget.

stories.title: Nem fejlesztették a BudapestGO-t, a parkolásnál több tízezren búcsút inthetnek a belengetett 25 százalékos kedvezménynek
stories.summary: Július 1-jén drágul a fővárosi parkolás és leszerelik az automatákat. Akinek van BKK-bérlete, az kedvezményt kapott volna az applikációban. Nem fog.

stories.title: Hungarian president signs law limiting PM's time in office
stories.summary: The amendment to the Fundamental Law, which was adopted in Parliament earlier in the week will make it impossible for Viktor Orbán to return to the office of Prime Minister.

stories.title: Hungarian government lifts ban on Ukrainian news outlets blocked by previous administration
stories.summary: The previous administration introduced the ban in response to Ukraine's decision to block several foreign news sites, including some Hungarian outlets which were part of the Orbán government's propaganda machinery.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs lemondott az MCC éléről
stories.summary: A Fidesz volt kampányfőnöke belátta, azzal tud a legtöbbet segíteni, ha nem vesz részt az alapítvány túléléséről szóló tárgyalásokon.

stories.title: Jogerősen elítélték a férfit, aki azért támadt egy társaságra, mert az antifasisztákból állt
stories.summary: Az ügyben annak idején az ügyészség nem emelt vádat, a sértetteknek azért pótmagánvádas eljárásban kellett kiharcolniuk az ítéletet.

stories.title: Magyar: Jövő héten kezdeményezik a hetes cikkes eljárás lezárását
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök az első uniós csúcstalálkozójáról tartott sajtótájékoztatót. Az ülésen a következő hétéves költségvetésről, Ukrajnáról, a Közel-Keletről és a versenyképességről is egyeztettek.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: Sierra Leone’s first lady refuses to condemn FGM without ‘reliable data’ on harms
stories.summary: Exclusive: health professionals, survivors and politicians voice concerns in open letter over comments by Fatima Maada Bio, who denies supporting the practice The first lady of Sierra Leone has denied that she supports female genital mutilation amid rising anger around her perceived approval of the practice. But in an exclusive response to the Guardian, Fatima Maada Bio, the wife of President Julius Maada Bio, also said she would not openly condemn FGM until she saw “reliable data” that the prac

stories.title: The Guardian view on defending Europe in a new era: collaboration is the key | Editorial
stories.summary: The recent abandonment of plans for a Franco-German fighter jet sent a disastrous signal. Strategic autonomy will be jointly achieved or not at all It has become a truism to assert that Europe needs to fast-track its own strategic independence in a volatile world. A recent paper from the European Council on Foreign Relations describes the continent’s leaders as grappling with “a ‘Schrödinger’s NATO’ moment, in which America remains formally inside the alliance while behaving as though it were no

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: EU, Ukraine start formal accession talks after Orban delay
stories.summary: The EU has formally opened the first stage of accession negotiations with Ukraine. This comes two years later than planned, as former Hungarian PM Viktor Orban was blocking the process prior to his election defeat.

stories.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
stories.summary: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

stories.title: US-Iran peace talks abruptly cancelled amid renewed Israeli strikes in Lebanon | First Thing
stories.summary: JD Vance lambasts Israeli critics of proposed deal but does not travel to Europe as planned. Plus, at 82 years old, Keith Richards still gives some of the best interview quotes in town Good morning. Talks due to take place today between the US ⁠⁠and ​Iran to implement a peace deal were abruptly cancelled . The White House said the US still looked forward to “beginning technical talks as soon as possible”. Hezbollah-linked media reported Tehran was delaying sending its delegation due to Israel’s

stories.title: Bill Pulte assumes role of US acting director of national intelligence
stories.summary: Head of Federal Housing Finance Agency who investigated Trump’s enemies takes over top intelligence role Timeline of Bill Pulte’s appointment as acting director of national intelligence Bill Pulte, the Federal Housing Finance Agency director, became the acting director of national intelligence (DNI) on Friday, following a tug-of-war between Donald Trump and Washington lawmakers over the short-term future of the cabinet intelligence post. Tulsi Gabbard, the outgoing DNI, initially planned to leav

stories.title: Iran–US peace talks postponed
stories.summary: Tehran and Washington have postponed the direct talks to hammer out the details of their tentative agreement to end the war in Iran, Switzerland’s Foreign Ministry announced on Friday. “The planned talks between the U.S., Iran, Qatar and Pakistan have been postponed … Preparatory work on the Bürgenstock [a luxury resort on a mountain next to Lake Lucerne] will continue. No further information can be provided at this time,” the ministry said in a brief statement . Vice President JD Vance on Thurs

stories.title: Costa on list of ‘undesirables’ targeted by Portuguese neo-Nazi group
stories.summary: European Council President António Costa was among the more than 100 politicians and public figures included in a list of “undesirables” targeted by a Portuguese neo-Nazi group, the country’s Public Prosecutor’s Office revealed Friday. Following its creation in 2018, the Lusitanian Armillary Movement (MAL) ― which authorities describe as a “far-right, nationalist, neo-Nazi, white supremacist, anti-establishment” movement ― began to compile a list of potential

stories.title: 'All of Lebanon must burn,' Israeli minister says after IDF reports four soldiers killed
stories.summary: Israel's military said the soldiers were killed "in combat" when their tank was hit during an operation near Kfar Tebnit.

stories.title: Lebanon air raids kill 18 as residents flee southern villages
stories.summary: Lebanon reported 18 deaths on 19 June after Israeli airstrikes hit several locations in the country's south and east, raising fresh concerns about the durability of a regional peace initiative announced earlier this week.

stories.title: UK’s top data regulator resigns, citing ‘poor judgement’
stories.summary: LONDON — John Edwards has resigned from his role running the U.K.’s data protection watchdog, citing “poor judgement” in interactions with colleagues. Edwards, who had helmed the U.K.’s Information Commission since 2022, had been subject to an independent workplace probe that began in February and concluded last week, finding there was “a case to answer.” The data watchdog said at the time that the next steps were for the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) to determine. In

stories.title: Burnham wins: Inside the Makerfield by-election
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is the new Labour MP for Makerfield, heading for parliament and, likely, Number 10. But what does his momentous win here on the outskirts of Wigan tell us about his chances of masterminding a wider Labour revival as prime minister? Host Patrick Baker has been in the constituency over the past few weeks, speaking to people who live and work in this curiously normal part of the country about the prospect of a Burnham premiership. On the campaign trail, Labour MP Peter Dowd reveals wha

stories.title: Italy’s Meloni fires back after Trump says she ‘begged’ him for a photo
stories.summary: The spat between Donald Trump and Giorgia Meloni escalated dramatically Friday, after the U.S. president said the Italian prime minister “begged” him for a photo at the G7 summit earlier this week. During a phone call with Italian media on Thursday night, Trump said Meloni was “probably happy I spoke to her. I didn’t have to,” adding that “she wanted a picture with me so badly. I wouldn’t have taken it, but I felt sorry for her.” The remarks appeared to contra

stories.title: Weatherwatch: Saharan dust drops microorganisms into European soil
stories.summary: Scientists are examining how ‘blood rain’ affects soil microbiome, with Portuguese vineyards a particular focus Dust events, when thousands of tonnes of fine sand whipped up from the Sahara are dumped over Europe, are becoming more intense. These sometimes produce “blood rain” that leaves visible red streaks, and while generally harmless, the dust is not sterile but brings a freight of microorganisms. One big concern is how imported microbes may affect the soil microbiome and impact agricultural

stories.title: Sydney’s ‘unbuildable motorway’ to be completed after two-year delay caused by sinkholes
stories.summary: State government and consortium strike deal to complete M6’s twin tunnels with minister insisting there is ‘no extra cost to NSW taxpayers’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tunnelling on Sydney’s $3.1bn M6 project, dubbed the potentially “ unbuildable motorway ”, will resume after the state government struck a deal with contractors, ending a two-year stalemate during which works were halted. Under the terms of the new agreement, the private consortium will absorb the

stories.title: Chalmers dismisses reports of ‘frosty’ relationship with Albanese – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed • Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast There were two small earthquakes in New South Wales this morning, according to Geoscience Australia. A 3.4 magnitude quake hit near Orange around 5.45am this morning, with at least 108 people reporting they felt the shakes. And a 3.2 magnitude temblor hit near Wilcannia two hours earlier. Just two people reported feeling that quake. If we’re given the choice between taking the easier path politically or doing

stories.title: KPMG leaked confidential Optus information and surveilled whistleblower’s laptop, inquiry hears
stories.summary: International firm owns up to breach of ethics after staff leaked confidential Optus information while bidding for telco contract Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast KPMG has admitted to another breach of ethics after its staff leaked Optus’ confidential information to colleagues bidding for an audit contract with Telstra. The consulting firm’s executives also surveilled a whistleblower’s laptop and dismissed the ind

stories.title: US-Iran talks abruptly called off after Israel and Hezbollah trade deadly attacks
stories.summary: JD Vance’s staff were at an airbase ready to fly to summit in Obbürgen before trip was suddenly cancelled Middle East crisis – live updates Talks due to take place on Friday between the US ⁠and ​Iran in Switzerland to implement a peace deal were cancelled as Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in south Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 18 people. The talks had been due to begin in the Swiss village of Obbürgen two days af

stories.title: Andy Burnham: our next Prime Minister? – podcast
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will return to Westminster as an MP, and potential challenger to Keir Starmer, after decisively beating Reform UK to win the Makerfield byelection. Kiran Stacey and Jessica Elgot chat through what happens next What would ‘change’ look like if Andy Burnham becomes prime minister? Support the Guardian: theguardian.com/politicspod Continue reading...

stories.title: Barack Obama says US is ‘worse off’ than before war with Iran
stories.summary: Former president nevertheless said in an interview that he was happy to see a ceasefire with Iran and hopes it holds Analysis: Iran peace deal makes clear how far US has been forced to retreat since 2025 Barack Obama has said that after 15 weeks of war with Iran , the US is now “worse off” than before the conflict started in February. “We’ve now fought a war, spent billions and billions of dollars, you know, put enormous strain on our military. A lot of people have died. And it feels like we’re

stories.title: India: Delhi court upholds government ban on Telegram
stories.summary: The ban on Telegram has prompted the most high-profile tussle between a tech giant and the Indian government this year.

stories.title: Guinea-Bissau: Opposition leader house arrest raises stakes
stories.summary: Opposition leader Domingos Simoes Pereira remains under house arrest months after the Guinea-Bissau coup, deepening a political crisis and raising tensions with international partners.

stories.title: In Trump's shadow, Vance becomes face of Iran deal
stories.summary: His fierce defence of the Iran plan amid mounting criticism comes as speculation intensifies about a possible 2028 presidential run.

stories.title: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
stories.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Trump’s Iran deal could place his legacy in the hands of Tehran
stories.summary: He lambasted Jimmy Carter during the 1980 hostage crisis; now Trump’s presidency could be similarly blemished It began with the fate of hostages. Donald Trump’s first recorded foray into politics was sparked by the 1979 takeover of the US embassy in Tehran, which saw 52 American diplomats held incommunicado for 444 days. Continue reading...

stories.title: Inside the city of grief hit hardest by Israel strikes on southern Lebanon
stories.summary: People in Nabatieh mourn the recent dead in religious ceremony held amid empty streets and shattered buildings As the procession wound its way through mounds of rubble, the crowd chanted and beat their chests, their lamentations echoed by the dull thud of shelling in the foothills just beyond the city. “This is the tragedy of Karbala, O Imam Hussein, look. This is the tragedy of Karbala,” the crowd cried in the opening procession of Ashura, in the city of Nabatieh, southern Lebanon. Continue rea

stories.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with

stories.title: Mainland Australia’s first suspected case of deadly H5N1 investigated after seabird tests positive for bird flu
stories.summary: Agriculture minister says no evidence of mass mortality or infection in poultry as WA authorities say a second bird is sick and being tested Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A migratory seabird found in southern Western Australia has tested positive for avian influenza with further testing under way to confirm if it is Australia’s first mainland case of the deadly H5N1 strain. The brown skua, a subantarctic species, was found at Cape Le Grand national park on Sunday a

stories.title: Venice’s new mayor seeks to raise day-tripper fee to up to €50
stories.summary: Simone Venturini says proposal aimed at discouraging arrivals in ‘periods of heightened tourist pressure’ Venice’s new mayor has said he hopes to raise a controversial entrance fee for day-trippers to the lagoon city to as much as €50 (£43). Simone Venturini, the rightwing former tourism councillor who was elected as mayor in late May, said the proposal was aimed at further discouraging arrivals “during periods of heightened tourist pressure”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Macron calls for vigilance as western Europe faces second heatwave of year
stories.summary: More than half of France’s population under severe weather warning with temperatures expected to exceed 40C More than half of France’s population is under a severe weather warning as large swathes of western Europe endure the second extreme heat event of the year with temperatures expected to exceed 40C (104F). The French president, Emmanuel Macron, called for “extreme vigilance from everyone”, asking citizens to “take care of our oldest and most vulnerable people” and follow government advice.

stories.title: St Kilda pier wins peak Victorian architecture award as judges praise playful and ‘deeply civic’ design
stories.summary: State government project among range of works celebrated for community-centred design that goes beyond utility The reimagined St Kilda pier has added more accolades to its burgeoning trophy cabinet, taking out some of the top gongs at the 2026 Australian Institute of Architects’ Victorian awards. The $53m Victorian government project redesigned by Jackson Clements Burrows Architects, alongside Site Office Landscape Architecture and AW Maritime, took home the Victorian architecture medal on Frida

stories.title: Bologna’s niche festival of forgotten films captures the streaming generation
stories.summary: Over 40 years, Italy’s Il Cinema Ritrovato – or ‘rediscovered cinema’ – has evolved into an influential international gathering Bologna will be transformed into an open-air museum of cinema on Saturday as a nine-day festival dedicated to restored, rediscovered and overlooked films – some dating back more than a century – gets under way in the northern Italian city. Now celebrating its 40th anniversary, Il Cinema Ritrovato, or “rediscovered cinema”, has evolved from its niche origins into an infl

stories.title: Midwives on frontline of childbirth deaths crisis denied visas for key summit
stories.summary: Outcry as experts from African and Asian countries – where mortality is highest – prevented from attending Portugal conference on prevention Visa rejections have threatened progress on mother and baby health after experts from struggling countries were barred from talks, global midwife leaders have said. Politicians, donors and UN agencies convened this week at the International Confederation of Midwives (ICM) congress in Lisbon, Portugal, a key conference to discuss the millions of avoidable mo

stories.title: Normal shipping will not resume in strait of Hormuz until 80 mines cleared
stories.summary: Tanker owner trade body says centre of strait will remain closed for ‘some time’, with vessels risking running aground by taking Omani route The centre of the strait of Hormuz is blocked with about 80 mines that will need clearing for normal shipping to resume, the independent tanker owner trade body has said. Several vessels began to exit the Gulf through the key maritime chokepoint on Thursday after the signing of a memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the US and Iran. Continue reading...

stories.title: Read a book? Join a club? Stare at a wall? Social media alternatives for under-16s
stories.summary: Amid UK government proposals for a ban, experts discuss what other activities might really serve children well When a Lancashire schoolgirl was asked what she would do if the social media ban for under-16s came into effect, her answer hit a national nerve: “Stare at a wall,” she deadpanned. The clip went viral, not least because it distilled a question many parents have been asking themselves about the consequences of the government’s plan. The answer, says Arran Wilson, of The Wildlife Trusts i

stories.title: Kidney cancer rates near Pfas factory in Lancashire a ‘major source of concern’
stories.summary: Experts cast doubt on conclusion of government-funded study of factory emitting forever chemicals near Blackpool Concerns have been raised about the conclusions drawn by a government-funded study that looked at rates of kidney cancer in the vicinity of a factory using forever chemicals near Blackpool. Pfoa, which is a known carcinogenic forever chemical that was globally banned in 2020, was emitted from the AGC Chemicals Europe factory in Thornton-Cleveleys, a town north of Blackpool, between th

stories.title: UK sexual predator who blackmailed Muslim men online jailed for 16 years
stories.summary: Police fear Waleed Saeed may have up to 70 more victims after campaign of entrapment, threat and extortion A sexual predator who targeted mostly Muslim males online for blackmail has been jailed for 16 years, with detectives fearing he may have up to 70 more victims. Waleed Saeed’s campaign of entrapment, threat and extortion started in 2018 and left one victim feeling suicidal because of the humiliation they felt. Anyone with information should call police on 101, quoting 5335/18JUNE2026. Conti

stories.title: Italy PM Meloni ‘stunned’ by Trump’s claims she begged him for a photo
stories.summary: US president provokes outrage as PM says he ‘totally invented’ story in interview with Italian media Italy’s prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, has said Donald Trump “totally invented” a story about her after the US president claimed she begged him to take a photo with her during the G7 summit. The two former allies had appeared to be getting their relationship back on track by having several one-to-ones on the sidelines at the gathering in Évian after falling out in April over the US-Israeli war i

stories.summary: US official tells Reuters news agency that ceasefire has now come into effect Inside the city of grief hit hardest by Israel strikes on southern Lebanon As the procession wound its way through mounds of rubble, the crowd chanted and beat their chests, their lamentations echoed by the dull thud of shelling in the foothills just beyond the city. stories.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
stories.summary: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the traffick

stories.title: Pete Hegseth accuses Nato countries of ‘free riding’ in combative address
stories.summary: US defence secretary addresses allies in latest attempt to get Europe to raise military budgets Pete Hegseth has announced a review of the US military presence across Europe, in a combative address to Nato allies where he threatened to cut force numbers in countries spending the least on defence. The US defence secretary, speaking at a meeting of Nato defence ministers in Brussels, accused some countries of “free riding” and others of being shameful for not allowing their airbases to be used by

stories.title: US probes Germany's 'persistent underpayment' for drugs
stories.summary: The US Trade Representative said a new probe would determine if Germany was a underpaying for pharmaceuticals.

stories.title: UK: Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham wins parliament seat
stories.summary: A by-election victory paves the way for Burnham to challenge PM Keir Starmer's leadership. But Starmer says, "I'm not going to walk away."

stories.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
stories.summary: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

stories.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
stories.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

stories.title: Israel, Hezbollah agree to ceasefire after morning flareup
stories.summary: US and regional officials say Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to a new ceasefire. This comes hours after four IDF soldiers were killed and Israel's government vowed retribution. DW has more.

stories.title: EU summit: Merz calls for cuts to 'too high' long-term budget
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the European Commission's long-term budget proposal for 2028 to 2034 was "far too high" and called for a new proposal.

stories.title: US: Stars and ex-leaders on hand to open Obama Center
stories.summary: The Barack Obama Presidential Center was opened in Chicago on Thursday in an event that was a celebration of democracy and an optimistic call to embrace "our shared responsibilities as citizens."

stories.title: Germany news: Heat alerts in effect for millions
stories.summary: Germany is bracing for a mix of high temperatures and thunderstorms on Friday. Repairs on the Bonn North Bridge will take another two years. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Months of war fundamentally change Iran-Gulf ties
stories.summary: The conflict in the Middle East has altered the strategic landscape between Tehran and Gulf states. Trust is lacking, but both sides recognize that they remain dependent on one another.

stories.title: Germany's post-communist Left Party is dreaming big
stories.summary: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

stories.title: Women tackling coal dust and other eco wins this week
stories.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

stories.title: The collapse of 'world order' as an opportunity for Asia?
stories.summary: In Europe—unlike in Asia—changes to decades of international norms and institutional systems are often interpreted as a loss. However, these changes may also mark the beginning of a new, more inclusive order.

stories.title: UK police release man arrested in crocodile-toddler incident
stories.summary: A man arrested after a toddler had to be rescued from a crocodile pen at a private zoo has been released on bail. Police say he was unfit for interview.

stories.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
stories.summary: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

stories.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

stories.title: Hegseth announces review of US forces in Europe as he lambasts NATO allies in Brussels meeting
stories.summary: Hegseth slammed Europe for prioritising what he called liberal ideals over practical defence needs as he lambasted the alliance’s defence chiefs in a rare appearance at the NATO HQ in Brussels.

stories.title: Do it at home too, women tell Japanese fans who cleaned World Cup stadium
stories.summary: Some see a double standard: Japanese men who clean in public while their wives do all the housework.

stories.title: Timelapse video shows ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz since reopening
stories.summary: A timelapse shows vessels moving through the Strait of Hormuz after the US and Iran signed an agreement ending their conflict and reopening one of the world's most important shipping routes.

stories.title: JD Vance cancels trip to Switzerland for planned US-Iran talks
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed an initial agreement aimed at bringing the Iran war to a close on Wednesday.

stories.title: Celebrations as Mexico becomes first nation to reach World Cup knockout stage
stories.summary: Thousands of jubilant Mexico supporters celebrated across the country on 18 June after their team became the first nation to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage with a 1-0 victory over South Korea.

stories.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
stories.summary: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

stories.title: Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport
stories.summary: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.

stories.title: Asia's richest man Ambani announces what could be India's biggest share sale
stories.summary: India's largest telecom operator is expected to raise around $4bn (£3.02bn), according to media reports.

stories.title: Boy, 12, wins hearts after trying to check sick chicken into Ethiopian hospital
stories.summary: Markos's beloved hen was not responding to any treatments - so he did the only thing he could think of.

stories.title: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi to stand trial for rape
stories.summary: French prosecutors confirm Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi will stand trial for rape, in accusations dating back to 2023.

stories.title: Obama moved to tears by wife Michelle's speech
stories.summary: The couple were opening a site in Chicago, which the former president described as a "celebration of community".

stories.title: Confusion in Strait of Hormuz amid reports Iran has reclosed the waterway
stories.summary: The Persian Gulf Strait Authority has announced that ships that submit "compliant transit requests" would be allowed to transit the Strait.

stories.title: New Russian strikes hit Kharkiv and Odesa, killing one and injuring children
stories.summary: New Russian strikes hit Ukraine's Kharkiv and Odesa regions on 19 June, killing one person, injuring children and adults, and damaging homes, vehicles and fuel facilities overnight.

stories.title: Italy's Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo at G7
stories.summary: The highly public exchange is an indication that their earlier close ties have frayed since Trump's decision to go to war with Iran.

stories.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
stories.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

stories.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
stories.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones were launched towards an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

stories.title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
stories.summary: France is putting artificial intelligence and robotics in the spotlight at VivaTech 2026, which opened on 17 June at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles.

stories.title: Cuba's President Miguel Díaz-Canel admits 'urgent changes' needed to overcome crisis
stories.summary: The oil blockade imposed by President Donald Trump in January has brought the island's already moribund economy to the brink of collapse, marked by power cuts sometimes lasting over 30 hours.

stories.title: Mangione's lawyers reverse course on psychiatric defence in state murder trial
stories.summary: The defence team reverse course a day after saying they would argue he suffered "extreme emotional disturbance".

stories.title: ‘We should send you to Moscow’: Belgian PM has a diplomatic mission for Costa
stories.summary: Bart De Wever knows who should represent Europe in any future peace talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin: António Costa. A late-night chat between POLITICO’s Hans von der Burchard and the Belgian prime minister after Thursday’s EU summit was interrupted by an unexpected passerby: European Council President Costa, backpack slung over his shoulder as he headed for the exit. Hilarious moment as I spoke to Bart De Wever about António Costa — who happened to just pass by. “I was just talking a

stories.title: Burnham says he wants to ensure Makerfield byelection ‘changes British politics’, in eve-of-poll speech – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog has now closed. See all our UK politics coverage here Andy Burnham may have trouble getting through to Keir Starmer if he tries ringing him after the Makerfield byelection to urge him to set a timetable for his departure. Burnham reportedly wants to call Starmer this weekend. (See 9.47am .) But, in his interview with Sky News, Starmer said: “I’m sure I’ll talk to Andy after the weekend.” If Starmer declines to take Burnham’s call, he may be following Ed Miliband’s example. In a Times s

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit oil refinery and residential building in Moscow – video
stories.summary: Flames and plumes of smoke filled the sky after a Moscow oil refinery was stuck in a Ukrainian drone attack. Up to 180 drones that were purportedly headed towards the Russian capital were shot down by air defence forces, according to Moscow's mayor. A high-rise residential building near the refinery was also damaged in the attack Moscow oil refinery on fire after ‘large-scale’ Ukrainian drone strike Europe live – latest updates Continue reading...

stories.title: Moment fuel storage lid flies off Moscow refinery during drone attack – video
stories.summary: Footage shows the moment an explosion at an oil refinery in Moscow caused a fuel storage lid to fly off. The explosion was caused by a Ukrainian drone attack where one of Moscow’s most important energy facilities was damaged for the second time this week Moscow oil refinery struck in Ukraine’s biggest air raid on city since start of war Continue reading...

stories.title: Rightwing MEPs chant 'send them back' after controversial deportation plans are passed – video
stories.summary: Several lawmakers celebrated a vote in the European parliament approving plans to increase deportations of undocumented people. Chants of 'send them back' spurred other MEPs to chant back 'shame on you'. The plans could lead to ICE-style immigration measures being implemented in Europe, which have been criticised by rights groups Continue reading...

stories.title: Moscow uses 'Russian Houses' in Africa to lure recruits into war in Ukraine, investigation shows
stories.summary: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

stories.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
stories.summary: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
stories.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

stories.title: UAE imposes limits on teen social media access
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has announced a social media ban for all children under 15. Platforms have one year to review and remove under-age accounts or face stiff penalties.

stories.title: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
stories.summary: While the human cost is clear, the Iranian regime has not just survived the war, it has been empowered.

stories.title: Lebanon: Tyre residents return to homes shattered by strikes
stories.summary: Lebanese families returning to Tyre found their homes in ruins on 18 June after Israeli strikes and voiced cautious optimism about a new US-Iran agreement.

stories.title: Parents of Serbia's teenage school shooter given jail terms in retrial
stories.summary: The boy was 13 when he killed eight girls, a boy and a school guard at Vladislav Ribnikar school in Belgrade in 2023.

stories.title: Iran's ayatollah says he approved US framework deal despite having 'different view'
stories.summary: Meanwhile, US Vice President JD Vance defended the deal, saying he would likely head to Switzerland for talks to turn it into a long-term agreement.

stories.title: Macron and Modi tour Indian pavilion at France's VivaTech
stories.summary: France highlighted its technology partnership with India on 18 June as President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister Narendra Modi toured VivaTech 2026 in Paris.

stories.title: Knicks revel in NBA victory parade as thousands of fans fill New York's streets

stories.title: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
stories.summary: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

stories.title: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
stories.summary: The US defence secretary's move follows a US decision to scale back its commitments to a high readiness force within the alliance.

stories.title: UK actress charged with importing meth worth almost A$300m into Australia
stories.summary: Emaa Hussen, 34, faces life in prison for allegedly trying to smuggle 320kg of meth hidden in bags of charcoal.

stories.title: Matolcsy Ádám dubaji cégéhez került egy Vörösmarty téri egykori MNB-s ingatlan
stories.summary: Az egykori Luxus Áruház épületében található irodának négy év alatt öt tulajdonosa volt.

stories.title: Lefújták Irán és az USA svájci béketárgyalását, miután Izrael újra összecsapott a Hezbollahhal
stories.summary: J. D. Vance alelnök utazott volna oda, most közölték, mégsem megy.

stories.title: Az lenne igazságos, ha azok fizetnék a természet helyreállítását, akik a fejlesztéseikkel tönkreteszik azt
stories.summary: Jó példa lehet az Egyesült Királyságban élő szabályozás, amely a beruházók megsarcolása helyett a természet helyreállítására kötelezi őket.

stories.title: Fideszes háttéremberek ellenőrizték volna, hogyan költi el a Krausz Ferenc-féle Élvonal Alapítvány a több százmilliárdos állami támogatást
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas tudós vezette tehetséggondozó szervezetnek közvetlenül a választás előtt ítélt oda 261 milliárdot a Fidesz-kormány, és ennek még a többszörösére tett ígéretet 25 évre. Az alapítvány felügyelőbizottságának mindhárom tagja erősen kötődik a volt kormánypárt holdudvarához, és Krausz májustól az Élvonal alatt működő nonprofit kutatócégénél is nagyon hasonló a helyzet.

stories.title: Szombattól javítják a ceglédi vasútvonalat, ha kész, gyorsabban járhatnak a vonatok
stories.summary: A munkák alatt nagyobb változásokra kell számítani az érintett járatokon.

stories.title: Bíróság elé kell állnia Hakíminek, a marokkóiak nemi erőszakkal vádolt sztárjának
stories.summary: A Paris Saint-Germain marokkói válogatott játékosa tagadja a vádakat.

stories.title: Másodjára is súlyos börtönbüntetést kaptak a belgrádi iskolában vérengző fiú szülei
stories.summary: Az ügynek nincs vége, az ügyész és a védő is fellebbezett.

stories.title: Mesék a strandtáskában: mi kerüljön bele és mit vegyél ki belőle? (x)
stories.summary: Nyár van – lehet, hogy végre több idő jut az olvasásra is? Amikor elmarad a reggeli rohanás, és az esték is hosszabbak, egy jó könyv igazi közös élménnyé válhat a családok számára. Már számos kutatás bizonyítja, hogy mekkora szerepe van a rendszeres olvasásnak a gyerekek fejlődésében. Épp ezért egyre több szülő keres tudatosan olyan nyári olvasmányt, ami nemcsak leköti a gyereket, hanem megszeretteti vele az olvasást is. A választás viszont nem egyszerű: a gyerekkönyvek világa soha nem volt enny

stories.title: Flekken helyett Wagyu és BBQ – robbanni látszik a hazai grillkultúra (x)
stories.summary: Bogrács, flekken, szalonnasütés – sokaknak ismerős és ma is gyakran űzött nyári foglalatosságok. Az elmúlt években azonban mind több teraszon, kertben, de akár belvárosi erkélyen feltűntek a faszenes grillezők, a talányos nevű kamadók, vagy éppen a titokzatos smokerek. És ezekben már közel sem az egyébként kiváló közértes zsírszalonna sül. A hazai grillrobbanás mélyére ástunk a prémium saját húsmárkát nemrég bevezető Kifli.hu szakértőinek segítségével.

stories.title: Irán legfelsőbb vezetője szerint Trump kétségbeesésből kötötte a megállapodást
stories.summary: Modzstaba Hameneinek eleinte fenntartásai voltak, de jóváhagyta a szándéknyilatkozatot.

stories.summary: Zohran Mamdani több száz kijelzőt vetne be, hogy a tömegek számára is elérhető legyen a foci-vb.

stories.title: Húsz százalékot zuhant a csúcsról a SpaceX részvénye, az átlagbefektetők már nullán állnak
stories.summary: A cég értéke a tőzsdei bevezetés után rövid időre majdnem elérte a 3 billió dollárt.

stories.title: Ritkán látni ilyet: négy amerikai elnök egy képen
stories.summary: Chicagóban megnyílt az Obama Központ. Többek között Stevie Wonder és Bono is elment az ünnepségre.

stories.title: Rengeteg ételt és áramot fogyasztunk, ami évente dollárbilliós terhet ró a környezetre egy tanulmány szerint
stories.summary: A világ legfelső 10 százalékába tartozó emberek átlagosan évente 2300–7500 dollárnyi (700 ezer és 2,3 millió forint közti) környezeti kárt okoznak fejenként.

stories.title: Új főigazgatót kap a Dél-pesti Centrumkórház július elsejétől
stories.summary: A dunaújvárosi Szent Pantaleon Kórház-Rendelőintézet jelenlegi főigazgatóját kérte fel az intézmény irányítására az egészségügyi miniszter.

stories.title: New York a felszínen tart, szuper ez a lebegés, de tudom nagyon egyedül is érezni magam
stories.summary: Köves Gábor évtizedek óta interjúzik világsztárokkal, írókkal, filmesekkel, színészekkel. Most megjelent az első regénye az Újlipótváros–Manhattan-tengelyről és egy magánnyomozóról, aki egykor Philip Rothhal is beszélgetett. Interjú azzal, aki folyton interjúzik.

stories.title: A világ legjobb lagzis zenekarát nem lehet otthagyni
stories.summary: Mástól lehet hamis, kioktató és kínos lenne ez a típusú műsor, David Byrne előadásában viszont új értelmet nyer, mit is jelent egy koncert ma, 2026-ban. Nehéz csúcspontot kiemelni az egészből, mert Byrne show-ja a nyughatatlan zenekarral számról számra változik át.

stories.title: Több százmilliárd forintos biznisz az ivószünet a vb-n
stories.summary: A mérkőzéseket közvetítő tévécsatornák iszonyatos pénzt kaszálnak a félidőnként háromperces leállással.

stories.title: Miért fizeti ki a kormány a diákmunkások bérének 75–100 százalékát?
stories.summary: Bőven van tér a foglalkoztatottság növelésére a diákok körében, ám könnyen lehet, hogy a bőkezű támogatás részben olyan pozíciókra megy, amelyek nélküle is létrejöttek volna.

stories.title: Tíz év után stabilizálódott a menekültek és menedékkérők létszáma Európában
stories.summary: 2024 és 2025 között már alig nőtt az Európában élő menedékkérők száma.

stories.summary: Azt írta, folyamatos és visszatérő panaszokat kapnak közgyűjteményi dolgozóktól.

stories.summary: Jó dolog a retinol, csak tudni kell használni, az impulzusvásárlásokat pedig érdemes kerülni – többek között erről, és a leggyakoribb bőrápolási tévhitekről beszélt Dr. Zsikla Kincső szakgyógyszerész az Alapozó adásában.

stories.title: Hét alkotmánybíró kizáratta magát Sulyok ügyéből
stories.summary: Pedig Polt Péterék már elkészítették az előterjesztést, ami támogatta volna az államfő beadványát. Így viszont az Alkotmánybíróság történetének egyik legnagyobb szervezeti konfliktusa jöhet.

stories.title: Svédasztalos menzákra áll át több szegedi iskola
stories.summary: Máshol már nagy sikere volt az új módszernek, amely számos előnnyel jár.

stories.title: Meloni kiakadt, mert Trump azt állította, hogy könyörgött neki egy közös képért a G7-csúcson
stories.summary: Trump szavai annyira felbosszantották Meloni kormányát, hogy az olasz külügyminiszter lemondta a jövő hétre tervezett amerikai útját.

stories.title: Polt levette Sulyok beadványát az Alkotmánybíróság napirendjéről
stories.summary: Elbukott az államfő elővágása, amivel ellehetetlenítette volna a saját eltávolítását.

stories.title: MOB-tagnak választották Szilágyi Liliána etikailag elmarasztalt apját, a bizottság szabályt módosítana
stories.summary: Reagált a MOB elnöke, aki a jövőben elkerülné az ilyen helyzeteket.

stories.title: Tűzszünetet kötött Izrael és a Hezbollah
stories.summary: A megállapodás pénteken helyi idő szerint 16:00-kor lépett hatályba.

stories.summary: Nagy Sándor alpolgármester szerint olyan képviselők fordultak szembe a javaslatával, akik korábban még támogatták.

stories.title: Egy cukiságbomba kismanul és öt ázsiai vadkutyakölyök esett át első állatorvosi vizsgálatán az állatkertben
stories.summary: Az állatkerti hagyományoknak megfelelően a kismanul nevét a látogatók szavazhatják meg a közeljövőben.

stories.title: 50 millió forintot kereshet a BKV Schwarzenegger filmforgatásán
stories.summary: Hétvégén nem fog járni a 2-es metró a Deák Ferenc tér és az Örs vezér tere között.

stories.title: Starmer warns against ‘looking backwards’ to Brexit after rivals back UK’s return to EU
stories.summary: Speaking at the G7 summit, prime minister says he stands by Labour’s manifesto pledge not to rejoin bloc The UK and the European Union should not waste time “looking backwards” to Brexit, Keir Starmer said on Wednesday, as he comes under pressure to reconsider rejoining the EU. The prime minister reaffirmed his government’s manifesto commitment to not re-enter the bloc, but said there had been “real progress” with the relationship, which was “slowly but surely building”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Rejoining customs union would not fix damage caused by Brexit, research finds
stories.summary: Exclusive: Economists find Brexit caused 12% depression in UK exports, most of which is due to leaving single market Brexit has depressed UK exports to the EU by 12%, and rejoining the customs union would undo only a fraction of the damage, research shared with the Guardian shows. With the UK’s future relationship with the bloc likely to feature prominently in a potential Labour leadership contest, the economists John Springford and Anton Spisak, of the Centre for European Reform, provide fresh

stories.title: Norway’s monarchy once seemed like a fairytale – recent crises have exposed its dark underbelly | Magnus Nome
stories.summary: The royals were always popular, but Crown Princess Mette-Marit’s Epstein connections and her son’s rape convictions have profoundly shaken public trust The Norwegian monarchy is in crisis. Not because its future queen is gravely ill , nor even because her son has this week been convicted of serious crimes , but because the institution’s greatest asset – public trust – has been eroded by a series of self-inflicted mistakes. Yesterday, it was announced that Norway’s crown princess, Mette-Marit, un

stories.title: From regime change to regime deal? How Tehran's political camps are reacting to leaked US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Iran's hardliners have framed the framework deal as a victory, even as Trump dismissed reports of a $300bn reconstruction fund and warned he would resume strikes if Tehran did not comply.

stories.title: Luigi Mangione to use psychiatric defence in health CEO murder trial, judge says
stories.summary: Brian Thompson was killed in December 2024 as he walked to a Manhattan hotel for UnitedHealth Group’s annual investor conference.

stories.title: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
stories.summary: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.

stories.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
stories.summary: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

stories.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
stories.summary: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

stories.title: Lawyer in high-profile Ugandan treason case charged with related offence
stories.summary: Uganda's military chief had boasted of "the hurt and pain" he would inflict on Erias Lukwago, who was arrested earlier this week.

stories.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
stories.summary: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

stories.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
stories.summary: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

stories.title: Is Russia heading for a fuel crisis?
stories.summary: Russia's fuel output is falling after Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries. Seasonal spikes in gasoline and diesel demand could push the system into a deeper crisis, experts say.

stories.title: Putin meets with ASEAN leaders in Russia as G7 vow 'unwavering' support for Ukraine
stories.summary: It comes after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with G7 leaders in France. Zelenskyy said on Wednesday they had agreed on the "additional strengthening of Ukraine’s air defense" and new measures against Moscow.

stories.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds
stories.summary: Canadian officials find structural defects in material used for hull and say firm failed to fully test ‘novel’ design Canadian safety officials have issued a damning report on the catastrophic final voyage of the Titan submersible, finding that the US company behind the expedition was overcome by “groupthink” and “confirmation bias” and failed to understand the profound risks confronting their largely untested craft. The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the A

stories.title: Cannabis commercialisation not decriminalisation drives up usage, study finds
stories.summary: Review reveals rise in users and rates of psychosis in countries where cannabis is sold commercially Decriminalising the possession of cannabis or strictly regulating access to the drug do not appear to drive up usage, but when the drug is sold commercially the number of users increases and more mental health problems are seen, a review has found. An international team analysed the dramatic shift in policies on cannabis between 2000 and 2025, including how the numbers of people taking the drug,

stories.title: Germany: Reports of antisemitism in 2025 remain high
stories.summary: The Federal Association of Departments for Research and Information on Antisemitism (RIAS) recorded over 8,700 cases they classify as antisemitic hostility in 2025, many of which were connected with the State of Israel.

stories.title: Nathaniel Brown: The left back Germany have been waiting for
stories.summary: A strong option at left back is overdue for Germany. Nathaniel Brown is quickly becoming a star at the World Cup.

stories.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
stories.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

stories.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
stories.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

stories.title: Read the full text of the 14-point US-Iran agreement as released by Tehran
stories.summary: After a draft agreement was leaked earlier this week, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian published Tehran's copy of the framework deal he and US President Donald Trump separately signed on Wednesday. Here are its contents.

stories.title: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon ahead of anticipated US-Iran framework deal
stories.summary: While violence has declined in Lebanon since a US-Iran agreement to end the Middle East war was announced on Monday, Israeli strikes on the south have killed at least five people since the deal, according to Lebanese state media.

stories.title: Trump welcomed to Versailles for dinner with Macron
stories.summary: Donald Trump was welcomed to the Palace of Versailles on Wednesday evening for a private reception and dinner hosted by French President Emmanuel Macron following the conclusion of the G7 summit in Evian.

stories.title: 'We had to get out of the way': The backlash over delivery robots
stories.summary: As the delivery vehicles increasing take to US streets, bans and protest groups are springing up.

stories.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
stories.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

stories.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
stories.summary: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

stories.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
stories.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

stories.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Palace of Versailles
stories.summary: The 14-point Memorandum of Understanding was signed at a post-G7 dinner in France.

stories.title: Teenager dies in horse-drawn carriage accident in New York
stories.summary: The horse bolted and toppled over after the carriage driver stepped down to take a picture of the passengers.

stories.title: Bird flu kills more than 75% of baby seals on remote Australian island, study finds
stories.summary: Scientists say 13,000 southern elephant seal pups on Heard Island have died of the H5N1 strain.

stories.title: Trump signs initial deal to end Iran war and open Strait of Hormuz as nuclear talks continue
stories.summary: Trump signed an initial framework deal to restore the pre-war status quo between Iran and the United States, inking an initial agreement towards ending a war he started with Israel on 28 February.

stories.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran deal at Palace of Versailles
stories.summary: Video released by the French presidency shows Donald Trump signing an agreement aimed at ending the war with Iran during a dinner at Versailles, with French President Emmanuel Macron seated beside him.

stories.title: UK: Tommy Robinson’s Oxford Union debate sparks protests and free speech backlash
stories.summary: Tommy Robinson’s appearance at the Oxford Union prompts large protests and reignites the UK debate over free expression and platforms for extremists.

stories.title: Watch: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Versailles
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran at the Palace of Versailles on 18 June, marking a significant diplomatic step aimed at easing the Middle East conflict that has unsettled the region since February.

stories.title: Lil Nas X says he's 'feeling better' after rehab and bipolar diagnosis
stories.summary: The Old Town Road star was arrested last year for attacking police officers and later entered rehab.

stories.title: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
stories.summary: The gunmen concealed their weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys while waiting for the victim outside Guayaquil airport.

stories.title: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.

stories.title: US and Iran sign deal, but who really won? Here's what to know
stories.summary: Trump signed the framework deal at Versailles while Pezeshkian signed in Tehran, pledging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and triggering 60 days of further talks. Analysts say the terms leave Tehran in a stronger position than the deal's framing suggests.

stories.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
stories.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.

stories.title: British man dies in paragliding accident in Spain
stories.summary: Authorities in Catalonia confirmed that the 63-year-old died in the Palau de Noguera area on Wednesday.

stories.title: Toronto celebrates as Ghana beat Panama with late World Cup goal
stories.summary: Canada became the backdrop for emotional celebrations as Ghana secured a late World Cup victory over Panama in Toronto. Supporters from both nations turned the city into a vibrant meeting point before the decisive Group L match.

stories.title: First Russian shadow fleet tanker enters Channel since Smyrtos boarding
stories.summary: Forwarder, a Russian-flagged ship which left port in Primorsk last week, entered the Channel on Wednesday evening.

stories.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
stories.summary: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices would rise or which products would be affected.

stories.title: Weapons, money and ships: How is this Iran deal different from others?
stories.summary: BBC Verify examines how the new deal between the US and Iran affects three key areas.

stories.title: How do Iranians and Israelis view the US-Iran deal?
stories.summary: Residents in Tehran and Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions to the US-Iran agreement, with some welcoming an end to the fighting while others questioned whether the deal would hold.

stories.title: Jury fails to reach verdict in Norwegian 'hitman' trial
stories.summary: Johannes Natland, 19, was remanded in custody and will now face a retrial.

stories.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
stories.summary: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.

stories.title: Germany and Uzbekistan deepen ties, President Steinmeier tells Euronews
stories.summary: Trade between Germany and Uzbekistan exceeded $1.4 billion (€1.2bn) last year, while German investment reached $2 billion (€1.7bn). The two countries are also expanding educational exchanges, labour mobility programmes and business partnerships.

<urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
